saw_palmetto_fronds.caption: Tough fans that survive fire.
saw_palmetto_fronds.description: Saw palmetto! These tough fan leaves can survive wildfires. The berries feed our bears and our birds.

scrub_hickory_nuts.caption: Only grow in Florida scrub.
scrub_hickory_nuts.description: Scrub hickory nuts! They only grow in our sandy scrub. When Pip buries and forgets these, baby trees grow!

sand_pine_cones.caption: Need fire to open.
sand_pine_cones.description: Sand pine cones! They need a wildfire to pop open and drop their seeds. Fire keeps our scrub alive.

florida_rosemary_cuttings.caption: First to grow back after fire.
florida_rosemary_cuttings.description: Florida rosemary! It's one of the very first plants to grow back after a scrub fire.

rusty_lyonia_flowers.caption: Spring blooms for pollinators.
rusty_lyonia_flowers.description: Rusty lyonia! Also called staggerbush. Its spring flowers feed bees and butterflies across the scrub.

chapman_oak_acorns.caption: A scrub jay's favorite snack.
chapman_oak_acorns.description: Chapman oak acorns! My favorite. I hide hundreds of these every fall and dig them up all winter.

flicker_feather.caption: Bright yellow shaft.
flicker_feather.description: A flicker feather! Look at the bright yellow underside. Flickers flash it when they fly.

pip_map.caption: Where the rare plants grow.
pip_map.description: Pip drew me a map! It shows where the rarest plants of the scrub live.
